claims.text: On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General determined that the epidemic of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the DRC and Uganda constitutes a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC).

In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, 20 from Uganda and one from France (linked to DRC); and 454 deaths including two from Uganda, have been reported. As of 1 July 2026, the Democratic Republic of the Congo had reported 1,460 confirmed cases including 452 deaths (case fatality ratio 30.9%) and 213 recovered; as of 2 July 2026 Uganda had reported 20 confirmed cases including two deaths and one probable case (deceased), with 16 recovered. Re-confirmed 10 July 2026: WHO's Disease Outbreak News page (DON 612, published 3 July 2026, referencing prior DON 608 of 19 June) remains the latest published report — no newer DON has superseded it — with cases reported from 36 health zones (Ituri 24/36, North Kivu 11/35, South Kivu 1/34) and Uganda reporting no new cases since 21 June.

UN News reported on 7 July 2026 that as of 4 July, the DRC government has recorded 1,561 confirmed cases, including 506 deaths and 254 people recovered; WHO's Dr Anne Ancia said the outbreak's true scale has not yet been fully established and that health authorities could not yet say the outbreak is stabilising. Re-checked 13 July 2026: no newer WHO Disease Outbreak News update has been published since DON 612 (3 July).

description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo declared its 17th Ebola outbreak — caused by Bundibugyo virus — on 15 May 2026, centred in Ituri Province. On 17 May 2026 the WHO Director-General declared the DRC and Uganda epidemic a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC). Confirmed cases and deaths have continued to climb into July, with a linked imported case confirmed in France; as of 4 July the DRC government reported over 1,500 cumulative cases and WHO cautioned the outbreak's true scale is not yet fully established.

impact.summary: As of 4 July 2026, the DRC government reported 1,561 confirmed cases and 506 deaths (254 recovered), per UN News; WHO's most recent published Disease Outbreak News report (DON 612, 3 July) had put the DRC+Uganda total at 1,481 cases and 454 deaths across 36 health zones, with Uganda reporting no new cases since 21 June. WHO staff caution the outbreak's true scale has not yet been fully established.

location.region: Ituri Province (Mongbwalu), DRC, with cases in Uganda and an imported case in France

name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) — DR Congo & Uganda
